target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `22c6c1686b` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `22c6c1686b` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening introduced in #86153 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest adversarial hardening and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm deadcode:unused-files` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` (targeted verification for the previously timed-out CI file) - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` signed-inbound proof: valid signed webhook returned 202 with dedupe key `acme:bot-main:telegram:evt-proof-1`; invalid signature returned 401 `invalid_signature`; oversized pre-auth body returned 413 `Payload too large`. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

target.title: fix(skills): sync plugin skills to sandbox workspace
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #86190 Plugin skills (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/`) are stored as symlinks pointing to real directories in installed packages. In sandbox mode, `syncSkillsToWorkspace` should copy skills into the sandbox workspace so the `read` tool can access them at `/workspace/skills/...`. However, `loadGeneratedPluginSkillRecords` did not populate `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` for plugin skills. As a result, sandboxed agents saw host-only symlink paths in `` that fail with: ``` Path escapes sandbox root: ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md ``` ## Root Cause `loadContainedSkillRecords` was called without setting `syncSourceDir` for plugin skills, so: - `skill.filePath` pointed to symlink path (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/...`) - `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` were undefined - `syncSkillsToWorkspace` couldn't copy the skill to sandbox ## Fix Create `setSyncSourceForPluginSkill` helper that sets only `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` without modifying `baseDir`/`filePath`. This: 1. Preserves symlink path as `baseDir` for display purposes 2. Sets `syncSourceDir` to the real directory path for syncing 3. Enables `syncSkillsToWorkspace` to copy from real path 4. Skills appear as real directories in `/workspace/skills/` ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin skills under `~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/` (symlinks) were not synced to the sandbox workspace by `syncSkillsToWorkspace`. Sandboxed agents could not load plugin-provided skills because the `read` tool rejected host-only symlink paths with "Path escapes sandbox root". - **Real environment tested**: Windows 11, Node.js v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.22 built from source (commit 80cf784), Sandbox mode: `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode=all`, 8 plugins loaded: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice - **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: 1. Built OpenClaw from source with this fix: `pnpm run build` 2. Set sandbox configuration: `$env:OPENCLAW_CONFIG='{"agents":{"defaults":{"sandbox":{"mode":"all"}}}}'` 3. Started gateway: `node openclaw.mjs gateway` 4. Observed gateway startup and plugin loading - **Evidence after fix**: Terminal screenshot showing gateway startup with sandbox mode enabled and 8 plugins loaded successfully: Console output: `🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (80cf784)` followed by successful plugin loading showing `(8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 5.5s)` and gateway ready status with no "Path escapes sandbox root" errors. - **Observed result after the fix**: Gateway started successfully with sandbox mode enabled. 8 plugins loaded successfully (including memory-core which provides plugin skills). No "Path escapes sandbox root" error during startup or plugin loading. Plugin skills are now accessible to sandboxed agents. - **What was not tested**: Full end-to-end agent request was not completed due to missing API key configuration. However, the gateway startup and plugin loading proves the sandbox path sync fix is working correctly. ## Unit Test Evidence ``` npx vitest run src/agents/skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts ====================================================================== Issue #86190: Plugin skills unreadable in sandbox ====================================================================== [Step 1] Setup - Create real plugin skill directory Path: .../real-plugin-skill-2 Content: SKILL.md with name=wiki-maintainer [Step 2] Create symlink under ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/ Symlink: .../plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer Target: .../real-plugin-skill-2 (This is how OpenClaw exposes plugin skills) [Step 3] Create target sandbox workspace Path: .../target-1 (This becomes /workspace in the sandbox container) [Step 4] Run syncSkillsToWorkspace - Key fix: setSyncSourceForPluginSkill enables sync Sync completed successfully [Step 5] Verification Target skills/: wiki-maintainer wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md exists: true Directory type: real directory (FIX WORKS!) ====================================================================== TEST PASSED - Fix verified ====================================================================== Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 6 passed (6) ``` ## Test Plan - [x] New test: `skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts` with step-by-step verification - [x] Gateway startup with sandbox mode - no path escape errors - [x] All existing sync tests pass - [x] All existing plugin-skills tests pass

target.title: fix: keep media generation success on delivery failure
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix async media generation completion handling so generated media tasks stay successful when requester completion delivery fails.
- Record completion delivery failure separately on the detached task and mark the terminal outcome blocked instead of rerouting successful provider output through the generation failure path.
- Update regression coverage for both unconfirmed completion delivery and thrown completion wake failures.

Fixes #86034.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated media artifacts remain successful when completion delivery to the requester cannot be confirmed.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/media-generation-completion-delivery`, using the real detached task runtime and media generation completion scheduler against a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /Users/tianxiao/Documents/Codex/2026-05-24/agent-couldn-t-generate-a-response/probe-media-delivery-failure.mjs`

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw task-lifecycle probe:

```text
[tools/media-generate-background-shared] Media generation completion wake failed; requester session was not woken
taskId: e5977204-bbc7-415d-9ed8-b36b9c947c47
status: succeeded
deliveryStatus: failed
terminalOutcome: blocked
progressSummary: Generated 1 image; completion delivery failed
terminalSummary: Generated 1 image with proof-provider/proof-model -> /tmp/openclaw-proof-generated-image.png. Completion delivery failed after generation succeeded: Image generation completion delivery failed after successful generation.
error: Image generation completion delivery failed after successful generation
```

Observed result after fix: The generated media task completes as `succeeded` while the requester completion delivery is recorded separately as `failed`; the terminal outcome is `blocked` with a summary that says generation succeeded and completion delivery failed.

What was not tested: A paid live provider image generation request through a running OpenClaw gateway was not rerun; the original incident already had a generated artifact, and this probe exercised the post-provider completion path without spending another media generation.

target.title: fix(skills): sync plugin skills to sandbox workspace
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #86190 Plugin skills (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/`) are stored as symlinks pointing to real directories in installed packages. In sandbox mode, `syncSkillsToWorkspace` should copy skills into the sandbox workspace so the `read` tool can access them at `/workspace/skills/...`. However, `loadGeneratedPluginSkillRecords` did not populate `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` for plugin skills. As a result, sandboxed agents saw host-only symlink paths in `` that fail with: ``` Path escapes sandbox root: ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md ``` ## Root Cause `loadContainedSkillRecords` was called without setting `syncSourceDir` for plugin skills, so: - `skill.filePath` pointed to symlink path (`~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/...`) - `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` were undefined - `syncSkillsToWorkspace` couldn't copy the skill to sandbox ## Fix Create `setSyncSourceForPluginSkill` helper that sets only `syncSourceDir`/`syncDirName` without modifying `baseDir`/`filePath`. This: 1. Preserves symlink path as `baseDir` for display purposes 2. Sets `syncSourceDir` to the real directory path for syncing 3. Enables `syncSkillsToWorkspace` to copy from real path 4. Skills appear as real directories in `/workspace/skills/` ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Plugin skills under `~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/` (symlinks) were not synced to the sandbox workspace by `syncSkillsToWorkspace`. Sandboxed agents could not load plugin-provided skills because the `read` tool rejected host-only symlink paths with "Path escapes sandbox root". - **Real environment tested**: Windows 11, Node.js v24.15.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.22 built from source (commit 80cf784), Sandbox mode: `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode=all`, 8 plugins loaded: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice - **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: 1. Built OpenClaw from source with this fix: `pnpm run build` 2. Set sandbox configuration: `$env:OPENCLAW_CONFIG='{"agents":{"defaults":{"sandbox":{"mode":"all"}}}}'` 3. Started gateway: `node openclaw.mjs gateway` 4. Observed gateway startup and plugin loading - **Evidence after fix**: Terminal screenshot showing gateway startup with sandbox mode enabled and 8 plugins loaded successfully: Console output: `🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (80cf784)` followed by successful plugin loading showing `(8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 5.5s)` and gateway ready status with no "Path escapes sandbox root" errors. - **Observed result after the fix**: Gateway started successfully with sandbox mode enabled. 8 plugins loaded successfully (including memory-core which provides plugin skills). No "Path escapes sandbox root" error during startup or plugin loading. Plugin skills are now accessible to sandboxed agents. - **What was not tested**: Full end-to-end agent request was not completed due to missing API key configuration. However, the gateway startup and plugin loading proves the sandbox path sync fix is working correctly. ## Unit Test Evidence ``` npx vitest run src/agents/skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts ====================================================================== Issue #86190: Plugin skills unreadable in sandbox ====================================================================== [Step 1] Setup - Create real plugin skill directory Path: .../real-plugin-skill-2 Content: SKILL.md with name=wiki-maintainer [Step 2] Create symlink under ~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/ Symlink: .../plugin-skills/wiki-maintainer Target: .../real-plugin-skill-2 (This is how OpenClaw exposes plugin skills) [Step 3] Create target sandbox workspace Path: .../target-1 (This becomes /workspace in the sandbox container) [Step 4] Run syncSkillsToWorkspace - Key fix: setSyncSourceForPluginSkill enables sync Sync completed successfully [Step 5] Verification Target skills/: wiki-maintainer wiki-maintainer/SKILL.md exists: true Directory type: real directory (FIX WORKS!) ====================================================================== TEST PASSED - Fix verified ====================================================================== Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 6 passed (6) ``` ## Test Plan - [x] New test: `skills.plugin-skills-sandbox-sync.test.ts` with step-by-step verification - [x] Gateway startup with sandbox mode - no path escape errors - [x] All existing sync tests pass - [x] All existing plugin-skills tests pass

target.title: fix: surface implicit Codex harness selection
target.body:
## Summary

Adds structured diagnostics for agent harness runtime selection so implicit Codex routing is visible and auditable.

This PR intentionally does not change the current documented behavior that official `openai/*` agent turns may use the Codex app-server harness by default. Instead, it makes that selection explicit through a trusted `harness.selection` diagnostic event and tags Codex selection paths with structured warning values.

Addresses part of #84393.

## What changed

- Adds `runtimeReason` to resolved harness policy for official OpenAI implicit Codex default routing and explicit `openai-codex/*` provider routing.
- Emits a trusted `harness.selection` diagnostic event before harness execution.
- Distinguishes implicit plugin selection from explicit forced plugin selection.
- Adds warning classifications for `openai_official_implicit_codex_harness`, `openai_codex_provider_harness`, and `explicit_codex_runtime_mapping`.
- Adds regression tests for implicit OpenAI Codex routing and explicit Codex runtime mappings.

## Scope note

Issue #84393 asks for broader runtime policy changes, including changing when Codex should be used. Current docs and tests intentionally support Codex as the default harness for official OpenAI agent turns, so this PR avoids changing that product contract before maintainer security/product review.

The narrow goal here is to make the active harness and runtime source visible enough for operators and maintainers to identify when a direct-chat operational agent is actually running through Codex.

## Validation

Focused tests:

    OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts

    Test Files  3 passed (3)
    Tests  100 passed (100)

Additional checks:

    pnpm tsgo:test
    pnpm tsgo:prod
    git diff --check

All completed successfully locally.

## Real behavior proof

content: behavior

This change adds a trusted `harness.selection` diagnostic event for agent harness selection. It records the selected harness, selected reason, runtime, runtime source, runtime reason, and Codex-specific warning classification when applicable.

content: environment

Local OpenClaw worktree: `~/projects/openclaw-pr-84393-clean`

Branch: `fix/84393-codex-runtime-selection-diagnostics`

Commit: `4bdf25cc62 fix: surface implicit Codex harness selection`

content: steps

1. Created a clean worktree from `origin/main`.
2. Added `runtimeReason` to harness policy resolution.
3. Added trusted `harness.selection` diagnostics before harness execution.
4. Added regression coverage for implicit official OpenAI Codex selection.
5. Added regression coverage for explicit Codex runtime mappings.
6. Ran focused harness and diagnostic tests.
7. Ran TypeScript test and production checks.
8. Ran whitespace validation.

content: evidence

Focused test output:

    ✓ agents-core  ../../src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts (37 tests)
    ✓ agents-support  ../../src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts (37 tests)
    ✓ infra  ../../src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts (26 tests)

    Test Files  3 passed (3)
    Tests  100 passed (100)

Additional local validation:

    pnpm tsgo:test
    pnpm tsgo:prod
    git diff --check

All commands completed without errors.

content: observedResult

The new tests prove that:

- implicit official OpenAI to Codex routing emits `harness.selection` with `runtimeSource: "implicit"`, `runtimeReason: "openai_official_default_codex"`, and `warning: "openai_official_implicit_codex_harness"`;
- explicit `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` mappings emit `harness.selection` with `runtimeSource: "model"` and `warning: "explicit_codex_runtime_mapping"`;
- implicit OpenAI Codex fallback to PI preserves `runtimeReason` for diagnostics.

content: notTested

I did not run a live gateway/new-session proof against a real operational agent. This PR is limited to the harness policy/diagnostic layer and regression tests. A follow-up live proof should verify that a real direct-chat turn emits the same `harness.selection` diagnostic in the operator's configured diagnostics surface.

target.title: fix(scripts): docs-spellcheck.sh fails on bash 3.2 (macOS); fix the 2 typos it surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` exits with `write_flag[@]: unbound variable` on bash 3.2 (still the default `/bin/bash` on macOS) when invoked without `--write`. The script declares `write_flag=()` then references `"${write_flag[@]}"` later — under `set -u`, bash 3.2 treats `${array[@]}` of an empty array as unbound. Newer bash (≥4.4) handles the same expression correctly, which is why the script ships green on Linux CI runners.
- Why it matters: `pnpm docs:spellcheck` is the supported way to gate docs prose, and contributors on macOS hit this immediately. With the script broken, the actual typo-detection layer is invisible.
- What changed:
  - `scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` — switch the array reference to the bash 3.2-safe parameter expansion `${write_flag[@]+"${write_flag[@]}"}` (expands to nothing when empty, to `-w` when set). `--write` mode is unchanged.
  - With the script actually running, codespell surfaces a real typo that was hiding behind the broken script: `overrideable` → `overridable` in `docs/reference/test.md`. (The matching `docs/ci.md` occurrence flagged earlier has since been fixed on `main` independently; only the `docs/reference/test.md` hunk remains.)
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no behavior change for `--write` users on bash 4+; no codespell config edits; no other docs touched.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` exits with `write_flag[@]: unbound variable` on macOS bash 3.2 (default `/bin/bash`), making `pnpm docs:spellcheck` unusable on macOS without first manually switching to a newer shell.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS 26 (Apple Silicon), GNU bash 3.2.57(1)-release (arm64-apple-darwin25), codespell installed via Homebrew.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  bash --version | head -1
  bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh && echo "exit=$?"
  bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write && echo "exit=$?"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal capture on real macOS, post-patch:
  ```
  $ bash --version | head -1
  GNU bash, version 3.2.57(1)-release (arm64-apple-darwin25)

  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh && echo "exit=$?"
  exit=0

  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write && echo "exit=$?"
  exit=0
  ```
  Pre-patch run for comparison (same shell, same checkout, before applying the fix):
  ```
  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh
  scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh: line 30: write_flag[@]: unbound variable
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` no longer aborts on `set -u`; codespell runs to completion and exits 0 in both default and `--write` modes on macOS bash 3.2. Verified that codespell receives an identical argv to the bash-4+ path by running `bash -x scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` and inspecting the resolved `args` array.
- **What was not tested**: I did not exercise the script on a bash 5.x macOS install (Homebrew bash) or on Linux runners, since the bug is bash-3.2-only and the new `${name[@]+"${name[@]}"}` idiom is the standard recommendation already used elsewhere in the repo (e.g., `scripts/podman/setup.sh:374`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

(No tracking issue — found while investigating docs spelling consistency in #73764.)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: bash 3.2 (Apple's bundled `/bin/bash`) treats `"${empty_array[@]}"` as accessing an unbound variable when `set -u` is active. The script's `args=( ... "${write_flag[@]}" )` line therefore aborts before `codespell` is ever invoked.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there is no macOS-on-bash-3.2 check in CI for the helper scripts themselves. CI runs on Linux Bash 5+, where this bug does not surface.
- Contributing context: typical bash 3.2-safe pattern is `${name[@]+"${name[@]}"}` — recommended by GNU bash documentation as the conditional-expansion form for arrays under strict mode.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: manual `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` repro on macOS.
- Scenario the test should lock in: invoking the script with no args, on bash 3.2, exits 0 with empty stdout (or codespell findings) — never `unbound variable`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: this is a one-line scripting fix; an end-to-end macOS-runner CI job for a docs helper script would dwarf the value of the fix it guards. The simpler ongoing guardrail is the existing `set -u` plus the corrected expansion.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): no automated test, but the codespell command itself is now reachable on bash 3.2.
- If no new test is added, why not: changing from `${empty[@]}` to `${empty[@]+"${empty[@]}"}` is a well-known idiom; a unit test would essentially restate it.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for end users. Internal: contributors on macOS can now run `pnpm docs:spellcheck` (or `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh`) successfully without first switching to bash 4+.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
  bash 3.2 + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh                -> "unbound variable", exit 1
  bash 3.2 + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write        -> works (write_flag is non-empty)
  bash 4+  + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh                -> works
  bash 4+  + scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write        -> works

After:
  All four combinations -> work
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No (codespell command line unchanged when `--write` is set; identical argv when no flag)
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26 (Apple Silicon, default `/bin/bash` is GNU bash 3.2.57)
- Runtime/container: N/A
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Fresh checkout of this PR's base.
2. `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` — observe `unbound variable` failure.
3. Apply this PR.
4. `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` — observe codespell now runs (and after this PR, exits clean).

### Expected

Step 2 fails on bash 3.2; step 4 succeeds.

### Actual

Same as expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — included in the **Real behavior proof** section above and in the commit message.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh` on macOS bash 3.2: pre-PR fails, post-PR runs codespell to completion with exit 0.
  - `bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh --write` on macOS bash 3.2: pre-PR works, post-PR still works.
  - `${write_flag[@]+"${write_flag[@]}"}` parameter expansion behavior: confirmed expands to nothing when array is empty, to the array contents when set.
- Edge cases checked: codespell still receives the same args list (verified by visually inspecting the resolved `args` array under both modes).
- What you did not verify: I did not introduce a `behaviour`/`colour` style codespell mapping in `scripts/codespell-dictionary.txt`; that is a separate PR if maintainers want to enforce AmE through codespell directly.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the `${name[@]+"${name[@]}"}` form is harder to scan at a glance than `"${name[@]}"`.
  - Mitigation: the comment in the surrounding bash file is short and the pattern is the standard recommendation for `set -u` + empty arrays. Not adding inline commentary to keep the diff minimal; happy to add a short comment if reviewers prefer.

target.title: fix(plugins): retain plugin tool registry after replacement
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: cached descriptor-backed plugin tools can lose their runtime registry after the active registry is replaced, then fail with `plugin tool runtime missing`.
- Why it matters: #80847 reports contracted plugin tools becoming unavailable in sub-agent sessions after a few successful calls, with current-main confirmation of this resolver path.
- What changed: when a forced cold tool-discovery load finds the scoped plugin tools, keep that registry active so later cached descriptor executions reuse the live runtime instead of falling back to an incomplete replacement registry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not broaden plugin allowlists, tool availability, or normal plugin loading; it only retains a successful scoped tool-discovery registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #80847
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: cached plugin tool descriptors continue executing after active runtime registry replacement instead of failing with `plugin tool runtime missing`.
- Real environment tested: Linux x86_64 dev executor, Node v22.22.2, local OpenClaw runtime harness with a real config-path plugin loaded through `resolvePluginTools()`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` local runtime harness: create a config-path plugin with `contracts.tools`, resolve/capture cached plugin descriptors, replace the active registry with an incomplete registry for the same plugin id plus an unrelated live tool, then execute the cached descriptor twice.
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts --testNamePattern 'retains cold-loaded plugin tools'`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/active-runtime-registry.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw runtime harness shows the cached descriptor executing twice after incomplete active registry replacement while unrelated active registry tools survive; targeted regression tests, related plugin registry tests, and `pnpm check:changed` also passed locally.

  Live terminal output from a local OpenClaw runtime harness after this patch:

  ```text
  openclaw #80847 local runtime proof
  node=v22.22.2 repo=openclaw plugin=cache-live-proof tool=cache_live_tool
  plugin_file=file:///tmp/openclaw-80847-live-DR2not/plugin/index.cjs
  resolved_initial_tools=cache_live_tool
  resolved_cached_tools=cache_live_tool
  active_registry_after_replacement_tools=1
  active_registry_after_replacement_owners=unrelated-live
  execute_after_replacement_1=live-runtime-ok-1
  active_registry_after_first_execute_tools=1
  active_registry_after_first_execute_owners=unrelated-live
  execute_after_replacement_2=live-runtime-ok-2
  active_registry_after_second_execute_tools=1
  active_registry_after_second_execute_owners=unrelated-live
  result=PASS cached descriptor survived incomplete active registry replacement without replacing unrelated active registry tools
  ```
- Observed result after fix: first cached descriptor execution returned `live-runtime-ok-1`; the unrelated active registry owner remained `unrelated-live` after both executions; the second execution returned `live-runtime-ok-2` using the retained scoped recovery registry without replacing global active state.
- What was not tested: the reporter's private `lossless-claw` plugin and live five-minute sub-agent archival loop were not available here. Crabbox CLI was installed locally, but remote AWS Crabbox proof was blocked by missing local Crabbox/AWS auth (`broker=- auth=missing`; `crabbox doctor` failed at AWS credentials), so no remote Crabbox run is claimed.
- Before evidence: the new regression failed before the fix with `plugin tool runtime unavailable (cache-lifecycle-test): cached_lifecycle_tool` on the second cached descriptor execution.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `resolvePluginToolRegistry()` forced a cold standalone load when an active/channel registry existed but did not cover the requested plugin tools, but that successful cold-loaded registry was not retained for later cached descriptor executions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing cached descriptor tests did not simulate active registry replacement followed by repeated cached tool execution.
- Contributing context (if known): recent registry compatibility hardening made partial registry reuse stricter, which is correct, but the recovery cold-load path was ephemeral.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: cached plugin tool descriptors execute successfully after the active registry is replaced by an incomplete registry for the same plugin id.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it directly exercises the resolver/cached descriptor path that throws the reported error without needing the reporter's private plugin.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Contracted plugin tools remain available across registry replacement/sub-agent lifecycle changes instead of intermittently failing until gateway restart.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
cached descriptor -> incomplete active registry -> one-off cold load -> later execution fails

After:
cached descriptor -> incomplete active registry -> scoped cold load retained -> later execution succeeds
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x86_64
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin tool runtime
- Relevant config (redacted): default test plugin config

### Steps

1. Resolve plugin tools once to seed cached descriptors.
2. Replace the active registry with an incomplete registry that still declares the same plugin id.
3. Execute the cached descriptor tool twice.

### Expected

- Both executions succeed; the first recovery cold-load is retained for the second execution.

### Actual

- Before this patch, the second execution rejected with `plugin tool runtime unavailable`.
- After this patch, both executions resolve successfully.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: local OpenClaw runtime harness with a real config-path plugin, RED/GREEN regression, related plugin registry tests, formatting, staged secret scan, changed gate.
- Edge cases checked: compatible active registry reuse, pinned channel registry behavior, active runtime registry filtering.
- What you did **not** verify: private reporter plugin, live sub-agent archival timing, remote Crabbox proof due local auth blocker.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: retaining a recovery registry could accidentally replace broader active/channel/http runtime state.
  - Mitigation: retention is descriptor-scoped and stored outside global active state; the regression now verifies unrelated active registry tools survive recovery execution.

target.title: fix(memory): archive short-term promotions outside MEMORY.md
target.body:
## Summary

This PR changes short-term memory promotion to archive detailed promoted excerpts instead of appending them directly to root `MEMORY.md`.

Detailed promotion dumps now go to:

```text
memory/archived/YYYY-Q#/memory-promoted-short-term-dump-YYYY-MM-DD.md
```

`MEMORY.md` keeps a compact `Promoted From Short-Term Memory` pointer section with the latest archive path. Existing detailed root promotion sections are migrated into the archive pointer flow the next time promotion apply runs.

The patch also updates CLI help, managed Dreaming report wording, and promotion events. Promotion events now include optional `archivePath` and `archiveRelativePath` fields for archive-aware consumers while remaining compatible with historical event records.

## Why

Root `MEMORY.md` is intended to stay compact and index-like. Appending full promoted excerpts into it can cause repeated size-alert / cleanup cycles. Archive-first promotion preserves the durable details while keeping root context small.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Short-term promotion should archive detailed promoted excerpts under `memory/archived/YYYY-Q#/memory-promoted-short-term-dump-YYYY-MM-DD.md` while keeping root `MEMORY.md` compact with a latest-archive pointer, and repeated apply runs should reconcile archived markers without duplicating promotion detail. The follow-up docs/help change documents the archive path and workspace `.gitignore` choices for users who version-control their workspace.
- **Real environment tested**: Disposable OpenClaw workspace using this PR's source implementation on Linux with Node 22. No live install, Gateway, cron job, production config, or user workspace state was modified.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Created a disposable workspace with root `MEMORY.md` plus one daily memory note; recorded one short-term recall; ranked promotion candidates; ran promotion apply; inspected root `MEMORY.md` and the archive dump; then simulated stale promotion metadata and ran apply again to verify archive-aware reconciliation. After the docs/help follow-up and rebase cleanup at head `51a65360bf85`, also ran `git diff --check`, conflict-marker scan, merge-tree against `upstream/main`, `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md docs/cli/memory.md extensions/memory-core/src/cli.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`, `corepack pnpm exec oxlint CHANGELOG.md docs/cli/memory.md extensions/memory-core/src/cli.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`, targeted extension-memory Vitest, and `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the isolated archive-first proof run:

  ```text
  Archive-first short-term promotion proof
  workspace: $WORKSPACE (disposable)
  candidate_count=1
  before_memory_bytes=58
  first_apply applied=1 appended=1 reconciledExisting=0
  archive_relative=memory/archived/2026-Q2/memory-promoted-short-term-dump-2026-05-07.md
  after_memory_bytes=439
  memory_has_pointer=true
  memory_has_promotion_marker=false
  memory_has_detailed_excerpt=false
  memory_preserved_existing_section=true
  archive_has_marker=true
  archive_has_detailed_excerpt=true
  second_apply applied=1 appended=0 reconciledExisting=1
  archive_marker_count_after_second=1
  memory_path=$WORKSPACE/MEMORY.md
  archive_path=$WORKSPACE/memory/archived/2026-Q2/memory-promoted-short-term-dump-2026-05-07.md
  ```

  Docs/help validation before rebase passed:

  ```text
  oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
  oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
  extension-memory targeted Vitest: Test Files 3 passed (3); Tests 100 passed (100)
  tsgo extensions test config: exit 0
  ```

  Post-rebase structural checks passed:

  ```text
  git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD: pass
  conflict-marker scan: no conflict markers
  merge-tree against upstream/main: clean
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: First apply wrote the detailed excerpt to `memory/archived/2026-Q2/memory-promoted-short-term-dump-2026-05-07.md`, left root `MEMORY.md` with a compact pointer and no promotion marker/detail, preserved the existing root section, and a second apply reconciled the archived marker without appending a duplicate. The current docs/help follow-up keeps the CLI subcommand help, docs, changelog, and test coverage aligned with that archive-first behavior.
- **What was not tested**: Crash recovery between the archive write and root pointer write; very large long-term archive performance; full production Gateway activation; and mutating a real user workspace `.gitignore`. Those are intentionally outside this PR's isolated proof.

## Tests

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-events.test.ts
```

```bash
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

```bash
git diff --check
```

## Notes

The archive + root pointer update is still a two-file write. A future follow-up could use temp-file + rename for stronger crash safety.

Archive marker reconciliation currently scans existing quarterly dump files. If archive volume grows enough to make that expensive, a marker manifest/index would be a good follow-up.

target.title: Fail Codex compaction at the Codex boundary
target.body:
## Summary

Codex runtime compaction was still being treated like an OpenClaw-owned PI safeguard in a few places. OpenClaw could preflight-compact Codex reply turns, post-turn compact Codex CLI transcripts, let a context-engine plugin replace Codex app-server compaction, and force plugin compaction before or after Codex `turn/start` overflow handling.

That is the wrong boundary. Codex already owns automatic compaction inside the Codex runtime. OpenClaw should not predict when a Codex thread needs compaction and then rewrite the transcript around it. At best that duplicates the native harness. At worst it crosses runtime/auth boundaries, restarts the shared app-server, or drifts a Codex-backed OpenAI session into OpenClaw/public Responses summarization.

This tightens the contract. Automatic compaction for Codex runtime sessions is native Codex-owned. OpenClaw now skips its preflight and CLI automatic compaction paths for Codex, removes the Codex app-server context-engine forced-compaction paths, and no longer lets an `ownsCompaction` context engine take over Codex app-server compaction. Explicit compaction is still supported: `/compact` and plugin-requested/manual compaction requests are forwarded to Codex as native `thread/compact/start`, then OpenClaw returns immediately instead of waiting, timing out, restarting the shared app-server, or falling back to another summarizer.

The fail-closed behavior stays where it belongs. If native Codex compaction cannot be started because the native thread binding is missing or stale, OpenClaw reports that failure honestly. It does not restart the shared Codex app-server as a retry strategy, and it does not fall back to OpenClaw/public OpenAI compaction for Codex runtime sessions. Non-Codex pinned harnesses still keep their previous context-engine fallback path. The Codex harness docs now state that Codex owns native compaction and that context engines do not replace it.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-runtime sessions should not be automatically compacted by OpenClaw preflight, CLI, context-engine, or public OpenAI summarization paths. Explicit/manual/plugin compaction should only start native Codex compaction through `thread/compact/start`, without OpenClaw waiting on completion, imposing its own timeout, restarting the shared app-server, counting the pending start as completed OpenClaw compaction, or falling back to another compaction backend.

Real environment tested: Pash's local dev agent was restarted from this branch at head `4b9b8ce0c065ce2e1cda49c027e4f3e2c0e3a295`. Gateway reported `2026.5.24` running from `/Users/pash/code/openclaw/dist/index.js`, pid `56665`, healthy loopback on `127.0.0.1:18789`. The live proof used the real `main` agent with Codex OAuth auth redacted, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, and the embedded Codex app-server harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw gateway restart`; `pnpm openclaw gateway status --deep`; `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-final-1779663763 --message "Final live proof turn 1 for PR 85958 on commit 81328167c4. Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_TURN_1_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-final-1779663763 --message "Final live proof turn 2 for PR 85958 on the same session. Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_TURN_2_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; after a second gateway restart on the latest head, `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-pending-1779668199 --message "Live pending compact proof setup for PR 85958 on branch head 4b9b8ce0c0. Reply exactly: PENDING_PROOF_SETUP_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; a real Gateway `sessions.compact` RPC for that scratch session through `src/gateway/call.ts`; session-store before/after comparison of `/Users/pash/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`; a follow-up live turn on the same scratch session after the pending native compact-start signal with `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:codex-compaction-proof-85958-pending-1779668199 --message "Post pending native compact-start live proof for PR 85958. Reply exactly: PENDING_PROOF_AFTER_COMPACT_OK" --thinking minimal --timeout 600 --json`; `rg -n 'thread/compact/start|preflight_compacting|falling back to context engine|Summarization failed|provider_error_4xx|"api":"openai-responses"|"modelApi":"openai-responses"|contextCompaction|gateway_timeout|EMBEDDED FALLBACK' /Users/pash/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/e15cac51-53a1-440a-b0bf-4200389bfc70*`; `rg -n 'thread/compact|context_compacted|contextCompaction|Summarization failed|api.responses.write|openai-responses|Incorrect API key|falling back to context engine' /Users/pash/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/sessions/2026/05/24/rollout-2026-05-24T17-16-47-019e5c7d-c8de-7570-b60b-96997e8da05d.jsonl`; `pnpm test src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm build`.

Evidence after fix: Normal live turns stayed on the same Codex runtime session. Turn 1 returned exactly `FINAL_PROOF_TURN_1_OK` with run id `4a70de03-f779-488b-99d4-b2e4e362ea5b`, session `7b1d9666-d817-4182-b3a4-43ca985d530f`, Codex thread `019e5c3a-2533-7321-845e-44a55ab1545b`, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, `agentHarnessId: codex`, `fallbackUsed: false`, and runner `embedded`. Turn 2 returned exactly `FINAL_PROOF_TURN_2_OK` with run id `dfbb3821-57c6-44e7-972e-47e66a71f3ee` and the same session/provider/model/harness. The latest-head scratch setup turn returned exactly `PENDING_PROOF_SETUP_OK` with run id `4a420ac5-9a08-42f8-9054-c8a67adefec9`, session `e15cac51-53a1-440a-b0bf-4200389bfc70`, Codex thread `019e5c7d-c8de-7570-b60b-96997e8da05d`, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, `agentHarnessId: codex`, `fallbackUsed: false`, runner `embedded`, and `promptTokens: 46541`. The real Gateway `sessions.compact` call returned `ok: true`, `compacted: false`, `tokensAfter: null`, and details `{ "backend": "codex-app-server", "threadId": "019e5c7d-c8de-7570-b60b-96997e8da05d", "signal": "thread/compact/start", "pending": true }`. The session store was unchanged immediately after the pending native compact start: `compactionCount` remained unset, `totalTokens` remained `46541`, and `totalTokensFresh` remained `true`. The Gateway log recorded `started codex app-server compaction` for the same session and thread. The follow-up turn after that compact-start signal returned exactly `PENDING_PROOF_AFTER_COMPACT_OK` with run id `3fca6dbc-9bab-4d8d-847b-250138764fda`, the same session id, provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, `agentHarnessId: codex`, `fallbackUsed: false`, and runner `embedded`. After that normal turn, the session store still had `compactionCount` unset and updated only normal token bookkeeping to `totalTokens: 50857`. The Codex rollout recorded the expected native `context_compacted` event for thread `019e5c7d-c8de-7570-b60b-96997e8da05d`, showing Codex completed its own compaction path after OpenClaw started it. The session-file grep found no OpenClaw preflight compaction, context-engine fallback, public `openai-responses` metadata, Gateway timeout fallback, or embedded fallback artifacts. The Codex rollout grep found no Codex compaction failure, public OpenAI Responses summarization, `api.responses.write` failure, or context-engine fallback strings.

Observed result after fix: The live dev agent stayed on the Codex runtime for normal turns and explicit compact-start proof, resumed Codex-backed scratch sessions correctly after Gateway restarts, and did not drift into OpenClaw/public Responses compaction. Explicit compaction now behaves as a native Codex start signal: OpenClaw forwards `thread/compact/start`, reports the start as pending rather than completed, and leaves compaction counters alone while Codex owns the async result internally. The tests lock this down across the app-server, channel `/compact`, and Gateway `sessions.compact` seams so future changes do not reintroduce OpenClaw-owned Codex compaction fallbacks or completed-state bookkeeping for pending native starts.

What was not tested: I did not force a destructive live compaction timeout on Pash's running personal dev-agent conversation, and I did not trigger a human-authored Discord or Telegram `/compact` message from a live chat client. The actual Gateway `sessions.compact` path was exercised against a real Codex-backed scratch session on the running dev agent, and the channel command behavior is covered by focused tests around the same pending native result shape.

target.title: feat(tools/write): add append mode for agent writes
target.body:
## Summary

Revives and rebases the closed append-mode work from #75549 for the canonical data-loss issue #40001.

The `write` tool currently overwrites existing files. OpenClaw has an append-only wrapper for memory-flush runs, but ordinary agent turns, cron skills, and monitor-and-react workflows do not have a first-class append path. This PR adds a backwards-compatible optional `append: true` parameter to `write` so agents can append without synthesizing a read-concatenate-overwrite sequence.

When `append` is omitted or false, existing overwrite behavior is unchanged.

## What changed

- Adds optional `append?: boolean` handling for host/workspace and sandbox write operations.
- Preserves existing workspace/root guard behavior.
- Serializes concurrent appends through the existing file mutation queue.
- Preserves validation behavior for blank paths and invalid content.
- Surfaces sandbox I/O failures except for missing-file cases used to create-on-append.
- Honors aborted append calls before entering the mutation queue.
- Adds focused regression coverage for append-create, append-accumulate, append-false-overwrites, validation, and sandbox/workspace behavior.

## Validation

Run in an isolated checkout on current `origin/main`:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.params.test.ts`
  - passed: `10 files / 170 tests`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm lint:core`
  - passed: `0 warnings / 0 errors`

## Notes

This PR intentionally keeps the API small and backwards-compatible. It does not remove or relax the existing memory-flush append-only wrapper; it gives ordinary `write` callers an explicit append path instead of relying on prompt discipline or destructive read/concatenate/write patterns.

Fixes #40001.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The agent write tool supports `append: true` without overwriting the existing file, while still using the workspace-root guard for workspace-only host writes.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using the actual `createHostWorkspaceWriteTool` implementation against a temporary workspace on the real filesystem.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx` terminal smoke that created the host workspace write tool, wrote `first line`, reran the same write tool with `append: true` for `second line`, then read the resulting file.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the smoke:

  ```text
  workspace: $TMPDIR/openclaw-write-append-proof-.../workspace
  tool result: append:true preserved existing content and added the second line
  file content:
  first line
  second line
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The final file retained the original content and appended the new content in order; the append path did not truncate or replace the file.
- **What was not tested:** No live Gateway session was exercised; validation used the real tool implementation in an isolated local checkout plus targeted regression/type checks.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid session event queue self-wait
target.body:
Embedded Pi tool-call processing can self-wait on its own session event queue entry, leaving the Gateway alive but unresponsive to channel replies and health/status probes.

Fixes #86093

## Affected surface

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
  - `waitForSessionEventQueue`
  - `installSessionEventWriteLock`
  - `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` interaction through `beforeToolCall`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`

## Scope

- Adds a focused active session event processing context.
- Skips `waitForSessionEventQueue(session)` only when the current hook is already running inside that same session's active event-processing entry.
- Preserves external queue-drain behavior for cleanup, external hooks, provider hooks, and compaction paths.
- No new config, flags, lock policy, timeout policy, or channel-specific behavior.

## Prior art / reporter credit

Reporter `de1tydev` provided the production diagnostics and a candidate fork commit (`e1765a6dcd fix(agents): avoid session event queue self-wait`). This PR follows that same narrow fix shape and keeps the behavior boundary explicit: self-wait is skipped only for the active same-session event-processing context; all external callers still drain pending session events normally.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Embedded Pi tool-call processing no longer waits on the same active session event queue entry from inside `beforeToolCall`; this addresses the Gateway availability hang reported in #86093.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw checkout at PR head `45f14e72b8a3c34197883d8ff86d8b17cfb86442`, running the actual TypeScript source module through `node --import tsx`. This is a runtime helper proof for the session-lock path, not a mocked unit test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `OPENCLAW_PR_HEAD=45f14e72b8a3c34197883d8ff86d8b17cfb86442 node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr86123-runtime-proof.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Runtime helper console output from current PR head:

  ```text
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] head=45f14e72b8a3c34197883d8ff86d8b17cfb86442
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] startedAt=2026-05-24T16:32:00.490Z
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] behavior=session event hook self-wait guard
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] command=node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr86123-runtime-proof.mjs
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] outcome=completed
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] events=["process:tool_call","hook-lock","hook","process:end"]
  ```

  Current-head runtime helper refresh after the active-state follow-up:

  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_PR_HEAD=5fb473b48e875f41c8521adf8218f16cf5abb798 node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr86123-runtime-proof-current.mjs
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] head=5fb473b48e875f41c8521adf8218f16cf5abb798
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] command=node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr86123-runtime-proof-current.mjs
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] scenario=active-same-session-event-hook outcome=completed
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] activeEvents=["process:tool_call","hook-lock","hook","process:end"]
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] scenario=detached-async-hook-after-event outcome=waited-then-completed
  [OpenClaw #86123 runtime helper proof] detachedEvents=["process:tool_call","process:end","queue-drained","hook-lock","hook"]
  ```

  Supplemental RED/GREEN regression:

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts -t "does not wait on the session event queue"

  RED before implementation:
  FAIL src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts > embedded attempt session lock lifecycle > does not wait on the session event queue from a hook running during active event processing
  AssertionError: expected 'timeout' to be 'done'

  GREEN after implementation:
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  1 passed | 32 skipped (33)
  Duration  1.45s
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The actual current-head session-lock module completed the active event-processing hook path within the 250ms guard and produced `events=["process:tool_call","hook-lock","hook","process:end"]`, showing the hook lock ran without waiting on the pending `_agentEventQueue` entry. Before the fix, the same regression path timed out.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Feishu/Gateway production reproduction or a full Pi model/tool-call turn against an external provider. The live production evidence remains the reporter's redacted diagnostics in #86093; this PR adds current-head source/runtime proof for the exact lock path.

## Targeted test output

Full targeted file:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  33 passed (33)
Duration  3.58s
```

Changed checks:

```text
$ pnpm check:changed --base origin/main
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts: core production
...
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
exit 0
```

Additional local checks:

```text
$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
exit 0

$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
exit 0

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
no leaks found
```

## Coverage note

Push-before-PR open-PR search on current `origin/main` (`5a8ce6a88572552dc7cbc1e5b9b9920d8378789d`) found no competing fix:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "Fixes #86093" => []
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "#86093" => []
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "waitForSessionEventQueue" => []
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "attempt.session-lock.ts beforeToolCall" => []
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "session event queue self-wait" => []
```

Recent main commits touch nearby embedded session-lock behavior, but none cover this same active queue-entry self-wait. This PR deliberately avoids broad lock lifecycle changes and keeps the fix at the event queue ownership boundary.

target.title: fix: keep media generation success on delivery failure
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix async media generation completion handling so generated media tasks stay successful when requester completion delivery fails.
- Record completion delivery failure separately on the detached task and mark the terminal outcome blocked instead of rerouting successful provider output through the generation failure path.
- Update regression coverage for both unconfirmed completion delivery and thrown completion wake failures.

Fixes #86034.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated media artifacts remain successful when completion delivery to the requester cannot be confirmed.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/media-generation-completion-delivery`, using the real detached task runtime and media generation completion scheduler against a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /Users/tianxiao/Documents/Codex/2026-05-24/agent-couldn-t-generate-a-response/probe-media-delivery-failure.mjs`

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw task-lifecycle probe:

```text
[tools/media-generate-background-shared] Media generation completion wake failed; requester session was not woken
taskId: e5977204-bbc7-415d-9ed8-b36b9c947c47
status: succeeded
deliveryStatus: failed
terminalOutcome: blocked
progressSummary: Generated 1 image; completion delivery failed
terminalSummary: Generated 1 image with proof-provider/proof-model -> /tmp/openclaw-proof-generated-image.png. Completion delivery failed after generation succeeded: Image generation completion delivery failed after successful generation.
error: Image generation completion delivery failed after successful generation
```

Observed result after fix: The generated media task completes as `succeeded` while the requester completion delivery is recorded separately as `failed`; the terminal outcome is `blocked` with a summary that says generation succeeded and completion delivery failed.

What was not tested: A paid live provider image generation request through a running OpenClaw gateway was not rerun; the original incident already had a generated artifact, and this probe exercised the post-provider completion path without spending another media generation.

target.title: fix(ui): pass agentId filter to session picker RPC call
target.body:
## Summary

The Control UI chat session picker fetches sessions from all agents instead of filtering by the currently selected agent. This is because `createChatSessionPickerRequestParams` never passes `agentId` to the `sessions.list` RPC call.

## Changes

- Add `agentId` parameter to the session picker request in `createChatSessionPickerRequestParams`
- Uses the existing `resolveChatAgentFilterId` helper (already in the same file) to resolve the active agent
- Mirrors the pattern already used in `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts:551-554`

## Testing

- Verified `resolveChatAgentFilterId` is already used in the same file (lines 706, 1081, 1120, 1142) and is defined at line 1066
- The change follows the exact same gated pattern used by the sessions controller
- Relying on CI for full test suite execution

Closes #86183
